methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rhoman Aerospace is a seed-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The valuation universe comprises 8 private peers within the sector, including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No public comparables exist in this category.

The peer-median method is not applicable due to insufficient narrowly-filtered comparables. Of the five same-sector peers identified, none satisfy simultaneous filters for stage alignment, adjacent-stage proximity, or similar valuation tier—each filter yielding fewer than three datapoints. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of business models, revenue profiles, and capital intensity across the broader Drones & Autonomous landscape.

Given these constraints, this analysis cannot produce a supported valuation range. A credible triangulation would require either: (1) expansion of the comparable set to include adjacent sectors with defensible methodology, (2) access to sufficient seed-stage financing data within Drones & Autonomous, or (3) application of forward-looking methods (venture capital method, DCF) supported by detailed operational and market assumptions. The primary uncertainty is the absence of a sufficient peer cohort, not market conditions or company fundamentals. Further diligence on Rhoman's specific unit economics and capital requirements would be necessary precursors to valuation.
